events.raw_text:

Thursday, May 7 · 5:00 PM to 7:30 PM PDT

Docusign Tower

999 3rd Ave #1000 · Seattle, WA

Seattle dbt Meetup, Late Spring 2026 Edition!

Hosted by Bishal G. and 3 others

dbt L. is a Super Organizer

Seattle dbt Meetup
5.0
23 reviews
Details

Welcome to the Seattle dbt Meetup, Late Spring Edition!
dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data. Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, AI, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation: https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy

Agenda

5:30 > Arrive, Eat, Drink, and be Merry (aka Socialize)
6:00 > Speaker 1, 2, 3, and 4 presentations
7:00 > Networking happy hour
7:30 > after hours at The Lodge Sports Bar (a block away, not dbt sponsored)

Location

Docusign Tower
999 3rd Ave, Suite 1000
Seattle, WA 98104
Instructions:
Meet at entrance and someone will assist escort you to the 10th floor for check-in
Parking Recommendations: Below building and street parking

Speaker Sessions:

1st Speakers:

William Weld, Principal Solutions Architect @ dbtLabs
Alexander Bogdanowicz Engineering Manager @ dbtLabs
Topic: dbt Fusion, dbt MCP, and what's new and exciting in dbt.

2nd Speaker:

Bishal Gupta, Docusign
Topic: dbt Fusion and MCP
Bishal is a Lead Data Engineer at DocuSign with over two decades of experience in data analytics and BI solutions.
He introduced dbt-core to DocuSign’s Product Data Science team and led its transition to dbt Cloud as the enterprise data platform. A strong advocate of DRY code, Bishal leverages dbt for building scalable, high-performance data models.

3rd Speaker:
Sundar Subramanyam. Docusign

Topic : AI Assisted Development with Cortex for dbt
Sundar is a data and analytics veteran with 15+ years of experience in data & analytics space. He is a problem solver at heart, passionate about building scalable data solutions and enabling self-serve analytics to drive business impact. He enjoys connecting the dots between data, technology, and strategy to create meaningful insights.

4th Speaker

Ryan Bordo, SWE, Netflix
Ryan is SWE at Netflix in their data platform team and actively integrating dbt core into our spark engine and maestro orchestrator using our workflow project management tool Dataflow.

About the Interactive Session
Join us for an interactive session where we dive into the latest experiments and problem-solving wins in dbt. Come ready to share the challenges you're tackling, new dbt features you've tested, and any recent breakthroughs in your projects. Stuck somewhere? Excited about a new approach? Let’s swap ideas and learn from each other's experiences. (And yes, there will be prizes!)
➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/🤝For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-seattle channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).
dbt is the standard in data transformation, used by over 40,000 organizations worldwide. Through the application of software engineering best practices like modularity, version control, testing, and documentation, dbt’s analytics engineering workflow helps teams work more efficiently to produce data the entire organization can trust. Learn more: https://www.getdbt.com/

Read more
Speaker
William Weld

Principal Resident Architect, dbt

events.raw_text:

Wed, Jun 18, 2025 · 5:30 PM to 8:00 PM PDT

Madrona Offices

999 Third Avenue, 33rd floor · Seattle, WA

How to find us
dbt Global Circuit: Seattle dbt Meetup
D

Hosted by dbt L. and Reuben M.

dbt L. is a Super Organizer

Seattle dbt Meetup
5.0
23 reviews
Details

In this meetup, we’ll dive into the latest tools and ideas we’ve been shipping — what they unlock, how they fit together, and why we built them the way we did.

This meetup is designed for builders, thinkers, and tinkerers — folks who want to roll up their sleeves (figuratively), learn from each other, and imagine what’s next for the analytics engineering stack.

Whether you’re curious about what’s new, want to see how others are approaching similar problems, or just want to ask hard questions — you’re in the right place.
🤝Organizer: dbt Labs
🏠Venue Host: Madrona Ventures (999 Third Avenue, 33rd Floor)
🍕Catering: TBD

📝Agenda

5:30 | Check in/Registration (30 min)
6:00 | Presentation: “For Builders, by Builders: The Latest Tools from dbt Labs” by Lukas Schulte (Director, Engineering @ dbt Labs, and Ex-SDF Labs)
6:30 - 8:00 | Reception

EVENT DETAILS:
To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation:** https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy

Unfortunately, there will be no parking validation available for guests parking in the building garage.

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/
🤝 Make sure to join the #local-seattle in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).

***

Since 2016, dbt Labs has been on a mission to help data practitioners create and disseminate organizational knowledge. dbt is the standard for AI-ready structured data. Powered by the dbt Fusion engine, it unlocks the performance, context, and trust that organizations need to scale analytics in the era of AI. Globally, more than 60,000 data teams use dbt, including those at Siemens, Roche and Condé Nast.
Learn more at getdbt.com, and follow dbt Labs on LinkedIn, X, Instagram, and YouTube.

Welcome to the Seattle dbt Meetup - Spring Edition!

dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data. Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation: https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy

Location

Address:
WeWork Office Space
107 Spring St.
Seattle, WA 98104

Meet at entrance and someone will assist get
Parking Recommendations: Below building and street parking

Speaker Sessions:

Wolfram Shulte, Cofounder, CTO at SDF Labs
Wolfram now Distinguished Software Engineer at dbt Labs, will update us on the SDF Lab's acquisition by dbt Labs and how SDF will accelerate the dbt developer experience. The two teams have been working side-by-side to bring SDF’s SQL comprehension technology into the hands of dbt users everywhere. SDF will be a massive upgrade to the very heart of the dbt user experience moving forward.

Bishal Gupta
Topic: Migrating Stored Procedures to dbt models
Bishal is a Data Science Engineering Tech Lead at DocuSign with over two decades of experience in data analytics and BI solutions.
He introduced dbt-core to DocuSign’s Product Data Science team and led its transition to dbt Cloud as the enterprise data platform. A strong advocate of DRY code, Bishal leverages dbt for building scalable, high-performance data models..

Sundar Subramanyam
Topic: Automating dbt unit tests development with Gen AI
Sundar is a data and analytics veteran with 15+ years of experience in data & analytics space. He is a problem solver at heart, passionate about building scalable data solutions and enabling self-serve analytics to drive business impact. He enjoys connecting the dots between data, technology, and strategy to create meaningful insights.

Mahalakshmi Sridharan
Topic: Automating dbt unit tests development with Gen AI
Mahalakshmi is a Data Engineer at Docusign, where she spends her time building data pipelines in the Azure cloud. In the past, she has worked on 0 to 1 development of highly scalable web APIs

About the Interactive Session
Join us for an interactive session where we dive into the latest experiments and problem-solving wins in dbt. Come ready to share the challenges you're tackling, new dbt features you've tested, and any recent breakthroughs in your projects. Stuck somewhere? Excited about a new approach? Let’s swap ideas and learn from each other's experiences. (And yes, there will be prizes!)

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/🤝For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-seattle channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).

events.page_title: Community AMA with Erica Louie (dbt Labs), Thu, Nov 21, 2024, 11:00 AM | Meetup
events.raw_text:

Thu, Nov 21, 2024 · 8:00 PM to 9:00 PM CET

Online event

This event has passed

Community AMA with Erica Louie (dbt Labs)
Network event

99 attendees from 54 groups hosting

D

Hosted by dbt L.

Super Organizer

Seattle dbt Meetup
5.0
23 reviews
Details

REGISTER HERE 👉 https://www.getdbt.com/resources/webinars/community-slack-ama

🚀 Hey Community! Are you ready to dive deep, get insights, and satisfy your curiosity?

We’re hosting monthly live Ask Me Anything events! It's the perfect platform to quench your curiosity and get to know each other better. Don't miss out on this opportunity to connect and learn together.

What to Expect:

Discussing trends in the analytics space
Sharing key takeaways from the recent events
Hearing your questions and thoughts

Speaker: Erica Louie, Data @ dbt Labs
Host: Bolaji Oyejide, Digital Community Mgr @ dbt Labs
Topic: Careers in data, building a data team, the dbt Semantic Layer, and scaling your dbt project
When: November 21st @ 2pm EST
Where: Virtual and in #dbt-community-merge

Erica was the second employee at dbt Labs, then called Fishtown Analytics and she's seen dbt grow, a lot. In this AMA, Erica will share more on her career in data, her strong opinions on building a data team, how she manages her team, the dbt Semantic Layer, scaling your dbt project, and more on her Substack.

Register now to get the link to watch and join the #dbt-community-ama channel in Slack to participate live!

Read more
Speaker
Erica Louie, dbt Labs

Second hire at dbt Labs, former Head of Data, and now leading the product data team (and previously worked for BuzzFeed's data team), Erica "ric" Louie proudly hails from a liberal arts background (studied political science + italian) and grew her career in data at dbt Labs starting from the ground-up. She firmly believes that anyone can be a data practitioner with the right mentors, resources (sans trivial data jargon), and a good sense of humor. While rethinking the philosophy behind data systems, operations, and teams is one of her favorite topics, she enjoys thinking and writing about the human experience, and how it intersects with the work we choose to do and who we choose to do it with.

Welcome to the Seattle dbt Meetup - Fall Edition!

dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data. Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation: https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy

Agenda

5:00 > Arrive, Eat, Drink, and be Merry (aka Socialize)
6:00 > Speaker presentations + Interactive Session
7:00 > Networking happy hour at Linda's Tavern (not dbt sponsored)

Location

Address: 1605 Boylston Ave, Seattle, WA 98122
Instructions: Entrance to the building next to Enterprise
Parking Recommendations: Harvard Garage across the street

Speakers
Wendy Grus + Hadif Mufti

About Foundations
Foundations is an invite-only, in-person community for tech founders and entrepreneurs based in Seattle. Our mission is to foster a thriving startup ecosystem in Seattle by bringing together experienced, exited, and early-stage founders (pre-seed to seed) to connect, collaborate, and share knowledge in a physical space in Capitol Hill.

About Hadif Mufti
Hi my name is Hadif and I’ve been a data engineer at Smartsheet for almost 2 years now where I primarily work on projects involving dbt and Airflow. Before Smartsheet, I studied math and computer science at the University of Washington where I graduated in 2023.

About Wendy Grus
Wendy Grus is a Senior Data Scientist at Snowflake, where she spends her time doing data analysis or writing data pipelines. She's worked in data analytics at tech and biotech companies for 12 years. She was a long-time organizer of Seattle PyLadies and an organizer for PyCascades 2024. She has previously spoken at PyCon US, PyData, Women in Data Science Puget Sound, and Data Umbrella.

About the Interactive Session
Join us for an interactive session where we dive into the latest experiments and problem-solving wins in dbt. Come ready to share the challenges you're tackling, new dbt features you've tested, and any recent breakthroughs in your projects. Stuck somewhere? Excited about a new approach? Let’s swap ideas and learn from each other's experiences. (And yes, there will be prizes!)

Thu, Mar 14, 2024 · 5:30 PM to 7:30 PM PDT

999 3rd Ave

999 3rd Ave · Seattle, WA

How to find us
Seattle dbt Meetup - Spring '24 Edition!

Hosted by Taylor D. and Anais V.

Seattle dbt Meetup
5.0
23 reviews
Details

Welcome to the Seattle dbt Meetup - Spring Edition! Let's cross our fingers that spring showers are over... bring on the sunshine!

dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

To attend, please read the Required Participation Language for In-Person Events with dbt Labs: https://bit.ly/3QIJXFb

Agenda

5:30 > Arrive, Eat, Drink, and be Merry (aka Socialize)
6:00 > Speaker 1, 2, & 3 presentations
7:00 > Networking happy hour
7:30 > after hours at The Lodge Sports Bar (a block away, not dbt sponsored)

Location

Address: 999 Third Avenue Seattle, WA 98104
Instructions: Meet in the lobby next to the dbt sign, we will have someone in the lobby to guide you to the meetup room!
Parking Recommendations: Parking lot within the Docusign Tower

Speakers
Bob Madden, Bishal Gupta, & Brandyn Lee

Bob Madden
Bob is a Lead Data Engineer who began working at DocuSign in 2020. Before DocuSign, he spent his career working on a diverse suite of Microsoft SQL Server projects at Microsoft Inc and A Place for Mom. When DocuSign adopted dbt Cloud a year ago, Bob worked closely on the implementation with a small team of engineers and has been responsible for establishing many of the core patterns and processes that are being used across the data engineering org today.

Brandyn Lee
Brandyn was an early dbt Core contributor and has over ten years experience in startups, analytics, and data science, currently serving as Head of Analytics at AgentSync. With a PhD in mathematics, they have a natural inclination to get into the details and are obsessed with ensuring analytics workflows are accurate, manageable, and repeatable, which is why dbt's testing framework is their favorite feature. In this talk, we'll hear from Brandyn on testing best practices; more specifically, how to interrogate the unspoken, implicit assumptions you're making when authoring dbt models and proactively encode those assumptions as tests.

Bishal Gupta
Bishal is a Data Science Engineering professional with over two decades of experience in building data analytics products and solutions for data-driven business intelligence platforms.He is currently working as a Senior Data Science Engineer at Docusign. He previously worked at Starbucks, Sur La Table, and Microsoft where he designed and developed data solutions in Microsoft BI Stack. Bishal initially introduced dbt-core in the Docusign Product Data Science and Analytics team and eventually moved to dbt cloud where it has been successfully adopted as the enterprise data platform to build robust and high performance data models and products. Bishal is a big proponent of DRY code and has found dbt to be promising for this engineering paradigm.

events.raw_text:

Thu, Aug 10, 2023 · 5:30 PM to 7:00 PM PDT

1425 4th Ave

1425 4th Ave · Seattle, WA

Seattle dbt Meetup (in-person)

Hosted by Anais V.

Seattle dbt Meetup
5.0
23 reviews
Details

Welcome to our SECOND dbt Meetup in Seattle!

dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

To attend, please read the Required Participation Language for In-Person Events with dbt Labs: https://bit.ly/3QIJXFb

Agenda

5:30 > Arrive, Eat, Drink, and be Merry (aka Socialize)
6:00 > Speaker 1 & 2 presentations
6:45 > Networking happy hour
7:15 > after hours at Frolik (across the street, not dbt sponsored)

Location

Address: 1425 4th Ave Seattle, WA, 98101 (10th floor)
Instructions: Meet in the lobby, we will have someone in the lobby help bring people to the 10th floor. If you arrive late and there is no one there, please message me
Parking Recommendations: USBC building (1 block away)
Read more
Speaker
Christian Lam & Dean Verhey

Christian Lam
Primarily working with Hadoop based data platforms since 2014, it wasn’t until early 2020 that Christian learned about dbt while listening to a podcast on a cold, dark, and wet bike commute to the office.

Fast forward a year to when the prospect of building out a data platform from scratch at Rad Power Bikes presented itself. He saw an exciting opportunity to show what small data teams are capable of while armed with the right mix of tools, with dbt as a key component of the stack.

Christian will delve into how his team leverages dbt to simplify and streamline the management of pii in a Snowflake data warehouse. Expect insights on dbt's role in pii field detection, segregation of sensitive data, and data masking.

Dean Verhey
Dean is an engineer at LaunchDarkly, working on LD's internal data platform in AWS, Snowflake, Airflow, and dbt-core. In the past 5 years he's seen a lot of dbt implementations across many different organizations, ranging from shoddy proof-of-concepts, to more sophisticated containerized and auto-scaling systems. You'll hear from Dean about some of those systems (good and bad!), how LaunchDarkly arrived at their current architecture, and some common pitfalls of running and maintaining dbt core in production.

When not working in dbt or chasing down obscure AWS errors, Dean can usually be found up in the mountains skiing in the winter, and trying to find more non-winter hobbies for the rest of the year.

Thu, Apr 27, 2023 · 5:30 PM to 7:30 PM PDT

2107 Elliott Ave #302

2107 Elliott Ave #302 · Seattle, WA

Seattle dbt Meetup - Kickoff Event!

Hosted by Taylor D. and Anais V.

Seattle dbt Meetup
5.0
23 reviews
Details

Welcome to the FIRST EVER dbt Meetup in Seattle!

To attend, please read the Required Participation Language for In-Person Events with dbt Labs: https://bit.ly/3QIJXFb

Agenda

5:30 > Arrive, Eat, Drink, and be Merry (aka Socialize)
6:00 > Speaker 1 & 2 presentations
6:45 > Networking happy hour
7:30 > after hours at the Black Cat Bar (across the street... not dbt sponsored)

Location

Address: 2107 Elliott Ave #302, Seattle, WA 98121 (Belltown Pioneer Collective, Elliott Court Event Room)
Instructions: Enter through the gate which will be unlocked and take the elevators to Suite 302 on the 3rd floor.
The space is located on Elliott Avenue between Lenora St and Bell St, just one block north of Pike Place Market.
Parking Recommendations
Read more
Speaker
Britton Stamper & Mira Winkel

Britton Stamper
Serving as the co-founder of Push.ai, 2 times as Head of Data and 3 years at Periscope Data, Britton has worked with hundreds of data teams on dbt and data deployments.

He's been a dbt power user since 2018, most recently building Push.ai as one of the pioneering products on top of dbt metrics and other semantic layers. We'll hear from Britton how you can shift your organization's mentality from dashboards to metrics and use this new metrics mentality to shape your organization: better align departments, drive transparency and consistency, and enable downstream consumers to move faster than before.

Mira Winkel
Being in the consulting space a touch over a year, Mira has already seen a number of dbt implementations at high profile companies. In that short amount of time she’s pushing the art-of -the-possible in dbt to satisfy the requests of her clients - exploring the graph object, redefining role based access control management, and accelerating data masking implementations utilizing dbt as the central control tool. Outside the office, she’s a certified sommelier and can tell you a thing or two about fine dining.
